title: khaya grandifoliola
intro: Khaya grandifoliola, also known as the African Mahogany or Kaya, is a species of evergreen tree native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimalarial properties. The health benefits of Khaya grandifoliola include treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as supporting immune function and wound healing. In herbal preparations, the bark, leaves, and seeds of the tree are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which are used to treat various health conditions.
